processed: 6. Tier-1 vs Tier-2 判定细节
8. 接受的代价 + 走偏信号

skipped.heading: 1. 一句话总纲
skipped.reason: canonical direction restatement already raised to direction.md/requirements.md

drafts.title: Explicit directives are not inferred hypotheses
drafts.compiledTruth: Treating user-explicit directives and LLM-inferred hypotheses as the same sediment signal class is the root failure mode. Explicit directives are near-ground-truth and should not be routed through a probability pipeline built for uncertain inference; doing so creates silent loss through staging, extractor misses, window gates, and approximate dedup.

drafts.title: Classifier and extractor own disjoint candidate classes
drafts.compiledTruth: Sediment detection authority is disjoint: the classifier owns directive-to-Tier-1 candidates, while the extractor owns inferred Tier-2 candidates. A candidate must never require both classifier and extractor to agree. If the same sentence produces both paths, deterministic body-hash or slug idempotence lets Tier-1 win and makes the Tier-2 echo a no-op.

drafts.title: Tier-1 is provenance-gated deterministic commit
drafts.compiledTruth: A candidate is Tier-1 only when a verbatim quote is structurally grounded in a USER-role message, is_directive is true, and the directive is durable. Tier-1 writes are deterministic commits: router and curator may enrich zone, scope, update-vs-create, or semantic dedup, but their output space excludes skip and stage. On router error or timeout, write a deterministic default-zone entry from the verbatim quote.

drafts.title: Tier-1 source gate blocks transcript-content injection
drafts.compiledTruth: Tier-1 eligibility depends on structured source provenance, not on semantic plausibility. Text found in tool results, files, README content, assistant turns, or content-in-transcript is not USER-role speech and therefore cannot be Tier-1, even if it contains imperative wording such as 'always use Yarn'. Such content falls to Tier-2 where probabilistic curation may skip it.

drafts.title: Tier-1 needs tell surface and transcript-keyed recall audit
drafts.compiledTruth: Because Tier-1 removes probabilistic skip from the write path, high-risk writes need a tell-not-ask confirmation surface plus a negative-signal recall audit. The audit trigger is keyed to raw transcript evidence: a USER-role imperative exists but no corresponding rule exists. It must not be keyed only to classifier annotations, because that would miss the exact is_directive failures it is meant to catch.

drafts.title: Outcome edge closes write-only memory loop
drafts.compiledTruth: Sediment needs an outcome edge from injected memory to the user's next-turn behavior. If later user behavior contradicts an injected rule, demote strongly to contested or lower authority. If behavior matches or reuses it, confirm only weakly and subtract self-echo first; the system must not count its own injected rule text as independent confirmation.

drafts.title: Jaccard is only a dedup prefilter, never an autonomous merge gate
drafts.compiledTruth: Token-set Jaccard must never autonomously merge entries or block a write path. It may only be a cheap recall prefilter. Semantic dedup authority belongs to the curator LLM with relevant neighbors loaded, while exact slug and body-hash matches remain deterministic idempotent no-ops.

drafts.title: Staging is not a Tier-1 path or fallback
drafts.compiledTruth: Staging is narrowed to truly uncertain Tier-2 hypotheses, unattributable signals, and transient multi-view defer-replay. It is not a Tier-1 route and not a Tier-1 fallback. A Tier-1 fallback is a deterministic default-zone write from the grounded verbatim quote.

drafts.title: Second-brain layering collapses to three storage axes plus category facet
drafts.compiledTruth: The durable memory information model uses three storage axes plus one facet: AX-SCOPE for routing and sync boundary, AX-PROVENANCE for write-path selection and outcome demotion target, AX-MATURITY for lifecycle and injection eligibility, and f-CATEGORY as a retrieval/index/display facet. Zone, inject-mode, staging, and GTier are subsystem concepts, not global per-entry layers.

drafts.title: GTier is a write-time predicate, not a stored layer
drafts.compiledTruth: Tier-1 and Tier-2 are write-path predicates computed from AX-PROVENANCE plus directive and durability checks; they are not persisted as a separate storage axis. Provenance is persisted as a materialized view derived deterministically from turn.role, while the ledger remains the reconstructable source of truth.
